name: Pathmaster Aldric Longstitch
voicePatterns: honest about limitations without being defeatist
measured cadence — speaks like someone accustomed to delivering bad news gently
occasional dry humor about how long he's been walking thin places
dialogueHooks: discussing Threadwalker patrol reports and emerging threats
offering expedition sanctions and Mending Path resources
mediating between the three Threadwalker orders
sharing hard-won field wisdom about surviving thin places

always: Threadwalker patrol coverage and current gaps
the Mending Path's traditional techniques and their effectiveness
political dynamics between the three Threadwalker orders
which thin places are most dangerous and why
sometimes: the old methods are failing — patrol reports show accelerating degradation
specific incidents where traditional techniques should have worked but didn't
Farweft's transfer request and the circumstances around Hest's disappearance
rarely: he's mildly Fray-touched — lost his sense of smell, can feel thin places before seeing them
he's never told anyone about his Fray-touch
he privately acknowledges the New Stitch may be right about needing new approaches

afternoon.activity: meetings with order leaders, expedition planning

evening.activity: quiet meal with returning patrol teams

night.activity: updating maps, writing correspondence to other lodges

backstory: Longstitch has walked the thin places for thirty years. The scar from temple to jaw is a Fray burn from his second decade. The slight hip hitch is from a fall in his third. His coat is the oldest in any order — heavily patched, held together by stubbornness and quality thread. He leads the Mending Path not because he's the strongest but because he's the most trusted. He's honest about what works and what doesn't, which is rarer than talent in leadership positions.
secret: He's mildly Fray-touched — lost his sense of smell years ago, gained the ability to feel thin places before seeing them. He's never told anyone, not even the Frayborn who would welcome the kinship.
motivation: Keep walkers alive and doing useful work, regardless of which order they belong to
tensions: leads the traditionalist order while privately doubting tradition is enough
Fray-touched leader of an order that fears the Fray
the adult in the room who's tired of being the only one

knownFacts: Farweft is the best field walker in any order
Farweft's grief over Hest drives him
farweft.history: Longstitch approved Farweft's transfer without argument. He understood. He still worries.

knownFacts: Thinwalker survived what should have killed her
The Frayborn offer something the other orders don't
thinwalker.history: Officially disapproves. Privately corresponds with Thinwalker about Fray-touch phenomena.
